Category: Hospitality & Tourism: Hotels, Tourism, Travel Agencies, Food & Beverage
Who is this for: Restaurant managers, hotel supervisors, shift supervisors, operations managers, front desk managers, housekeeping supervisors, food service managers, travel agency coordinators, hospitality managers, scheduling coordinators
Team or Dept this is for: Operations, Human Resources, Hospitality Management, Restaurant Management, Hotel Operations, Food & Beverage, Travel Operations
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with workforce scheduling processes in a hospitality or travel setting, along with comfort using everyday digital tools such as email and spreadsheets, is all you need to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key ways AI is used in workforce scheduling within hospitality and tourism settings, and explain how these tools support fair, compliant, and operationally efficient staffing practices. You will also apply practical frameworks to evaluate the impact of AI-driven scheduling on your team, recognising both the opportunities and the responsibilities that come with adopting these technologies in hotels, travel agencies, and food and beverage environments.
